content: Canvas/Moodle quiz availability windows use available_from/until distinct from the timer duration: a student can only START within the window, and if they start close to the 'until' boundary, they get whichever is shorter — remaining window time or the configured duration (Canvas explicitly truncates: a 60-min quiz started 30 min before close only gets 30 min). Once submitted or once 'until' passes, the attempt auto-closes; already-in-progress attempts are force-submitted at the window boundary, not merely blocked from new starts. Moodle: omitting open date means always-available-from-start; omitting close means always-available-until-closed; both belong to the exam record, independent of any individual attempt's timer.
